concerns.text: Task T027 implements 'Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM)' while spec FR-005 explicitly mandates 'Wilcoxon Signed-Rank tests'. The plan.md acknowledges this change as a 'superseding' methodological correction, but the tasks document fails to reflect the required spec amendment. Implementing LMM without a corresponding FR update violates FR-012 (no requirement-weakening) and creates a semantic contradiction where the task satisfies a requirement (FR-005) it does not actually implement.

Task T015 implements matching using 'block-level metrics and repo-level covariates (stars, age)'. Spec FR-008 strictly limits covariates to 'initial cyclomatic complexity and project size (LOC)'. The inclusion of 'stars' and 'age' constitutes a silent expansion of the experimental design not authorized by the spec, potentially introducing uncontrolled confounding variables and violating the strict definition of the matching protocol in FR-008.

Task T028 applies Benjamini-Hochberg correction to the LMM analysis. Spec FR-005 mandates this correction specifically for 'Wilcoxon Signed-Rank tests'. While statistically valid for LMM, the task assumes the correction logic transfers without verification or spec update. This creates a traceability gap where the acceptance criterion (FDR control) is met via a different statistical mechanism than the one specified in FR-005.
